features.title: Trellis Engineering project structure
definition_of_done: AGENTS.md exists and defines startup, work, completion, boundary, and quality protocols
.trellis/spec/fluxpost contains project brief, feature state, progress, handoff, decisions, verification, pitfalls, and architecture rules
.trellis/verification/check.ps1 runs real baseline verification without external production service calls
README explains Trellis recovery and baseline verification
evidence: 2026-07-01 Trellis CLI 0.6.5 installed and initialized with Codex integration. Active project context moved to .trellis/spec/fluxpost, baseline scripts moved to .trellis/verification, and old docs/harness plus scripts/harness directories were renamed to disabled archives.
2026-06-17 Trellis context budget migration completed: AGENTS.md startup reads status.md, feature_list.json, and rules.md; handoff/progress are on-demand history with TRELLIS-LATEST markers.
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Initialized as the canonical long-term AI collaboration context.

features.title: Keyword crawl and content pool
definition_of_done: Users can submit platform, keyword, count, and optional filters
TikHub results are normalized and written to data/content-pool.json
Crawled items expose metrics, media URLs, crawled time, and published time when available
evidence: Source files present: src/app/api/crawl/jobs/route.ts, src/lib/tikhub.ts, src/lib/content-pool.ts

2026-06-16 Dongchedi source-link/ID import added through src/lib/dongchedi.ts and src/lib/source-link-import.ts, with advanced/simple link-mode UI/API wiring, generated-post/review labels, and .trellis/verification/dongchedi_import_check.mjs in the baseline; verification passed without live Dongchedi/TikHub/OpenAI/image-provider/Feishu production calls
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: External TikHub behavior is not part of default baseline verification.

features.title: Crawled media cache, video frames, and video transcription
definition_of_done: Crawled images and videos can be cached under public/media/crawl
Downloaded videos can produce cover, interval, or scene-change frames
Failures are visible to the caller and do not delete unrelated media
Configured Ark video transcription can merge speech-to-text output into the source body before rewrite
evidence: 2026-07-01 Douyin video ranking uses resolution, bitrate, URL br/dimension hints, and CDN/download hints; bit_rate[] extraction covers play_addr/play_addr_265/play_addr_h264/download_addr, and content-pool media caching supports explicit force video refresh. video_quality_selection_check.mjs, video_download_fallback_check.mjs, full baseline, and local:restart passed without live provider calls.

2026-07-01 video high-frame selection now samples across cached videos, adds local quality scoring, filters obvious bad frames, and can apply OpenAI-compatible visual review without requiring complete-car framing; .trellis/verification/video_frame_policy_check.mjs plus the full Trellis baseline passed without live model calls.

2026-06-25 video transcription now extracts MP3 audio from cached videos with ffmpeg, uploads the MP3 through Ark Files, calls Ark Responses with input_audio.file_id, stores successful text on videoTranscript, and appends it to contentText before rewrite; .trellis/verification/video_transcription_check.mjs and npx --no-install tsc --noEmit passed without live Ark calls.
features.notes: Requires system ffmpeg on PATH for video frames and MP3 audio extraction. Live transcription requires ARK_API_KEY or the existing VOLCENGINE_ASR_APP_KEY alias, plus optional Ark model/prompt/audio-size/timeout variables; default verification does not call the external Ark service.

features.title: Batch content production
definition_of_done: Users can select source items and material paths
Each source item gets an independent production task
Generated drafts include title, body, image prompt, image task choices, and review status
evidence: Source files present: src/app/api/production/batches/route.ts, src/lib/batch-production.ts, src/lib/openai.ts
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: OpenAI-compatible external calls are not part of default baseline verification.

features.title: Review and Feishu publishing
definition_of_done: Users can manually patch or prompt-edit a generated post
Approved posts can be staged as Feishu payloads
Configured Feishu CLI can publish using the project wrapper
evidence: Source files present: src/app/api/review/route.ts, src/app/api/publish/feishu/route.ts, src/lib/feishu-cli.ts

2026-06-12 verification passed for /review: npx --no-install tsc --noEmit; npm run lint with existing 3 warnings only; npm run build; powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .trellis/verification/check.ps1; npm run local:restart
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Ready for a real publish test with one approved generated post that has final local images; no real Base record creation has been executed in this verification. Feishu IM notification requires FEISHU_NOTIFY_CHAT_ID or FEISHU_NOTIFY_USER_ID plus bot message permission.

features.title: Feishu distribution audit write-back
definition_of_done: Users can bulk input Feishu record numbers from a signed-in browser session
The app can judge whether each record is distributable to other Xiaohongshu accounts
The app scores each record with a cheat-on-content-style pre-publication score and prediction without letting score force the distribution decision
Approved and blocked decisions are written back to the Feishu single-select field 是否分发, and the final numeric score is written to 内容评分
Default verification covers the tool without triggering live Feishu writes
evidence: Source files present: src/lib/distribution-check.ts, src/app/api/distribution-check/route.ts, and src/app/distribution-check/page.tsx

2026-06-17 distribution audit was upgraded to durable queue/progress mode: /distribution-check POST enqueues owner-scoped jobs, GET polls job/list progress, distribution_check_jobs stores progress/results, dedicated distribution concurrency pools isolate large audits from collection/generation work, and full Trellis baseline passed without live Feishu writes.
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Ready for a small intentional live validation with real 编号 values. No live Feishu write was triggered by automated verification.

features.title: Execution activity log
definition_of_done: Backend operations record activity entries
Frontend can read recent activity
Users can clear the activity log from the API
evidence: Source files present: src/app/api/activity/route.ts, src/lib/activity-log.ts
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Stored in SQLite table execution_logs; legacy data/execution-log.json is retained as a migration artifact.

features.title: SQLite runtime persistence
definition_of_done: Runtime stores use SQLite instead of JSON files as the primary write path
Existing JSON runtime data can migrate into SQLite without deleting legacy files
Baseline verification validates SQLite schema and store availability
evidence: Source file present: src/lib/database.ts
2026-06-02 passed: npm run lint; npx --no-install tsc --noEmit; npm run build; powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .trellis/verification/check.ps1
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Uses Node.js built-in node:sqlite, which requires Node 24+ and emits an expected experimental warning.

features.title: PostgreSQL runtime storage and migration
definition_of_done: Runtime storage can use PostgreSQL when DATABASE_URL is configured
The app continues to use SQLite by default when DATABASE_URL is not configured
A PostgreSQL schema migration exists for the current runtime tables
A SQLite-to-PostgreSQL migration script can copy current runtime rows without moving media binaries
Baseline verification checks the PostgreSQL schema without requiring a live PostgreSQL server
evidence: 2026-06-04 passed: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .trellis/verification/check.ps1
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Live local migration is complete. Current JSONB-table design preserves existing behavior; high-volume queue tables and SKIP LOCKED workers remain a follow-up.

features.title: One-command Ubuntu VPS deployment
definition_of_done: A fresh Ubuntu 24.04 VPS can bootstrap from GitHub with generated root-only base secrets
Pre-domain deployment starts only app and PostgreSQL on a loopback port for SSH-tunnel access
A validated DNS hostname can enable Caddy HTTPS through one command
Routine updates preserve PostgreSQL, advanced configuration, runtime data, media, and Caddy named volumes
Default verification covers Compose parsing, Bash syntax, destructive-command absence, and private/HTTPS/legacy deployment plans without live infrastructure changes
Every production code fix passes the isolated full candidate gate and task scenario on staging 104 before the unchanged full SHA is promoted to production 38
evidence: 2026-07-15 repository implementation includes parameterized compose.yaml, vps-bootstrap.sh, vps-deploy.sh --check, vps-enable-domain.sh, beginner deployment documentation, and .trellis/verification/vps_deployment_check.mjs. Private/HTTPS/legacy plan execution, Bash syntax, YAML parsing, focused checks, type-check, lint, build, and full baseline passed without a live VPS install.
features.notes: Ready for review because the deterministic contracts pass; mark done only after one intentional clean Ubuntu 24.04 installation and later domain/HTTPS enablement are verified.

features.title: Modular content, production, and material workspaces
definition_of_done: The web UI exposes separate top-level modules for content pool management, production, and material library management
Content pool items can be created manually, edited, and deleted through local API routes
Generated posts are persisted, selectable, editable, deletable, and can be regenerated as versions
Material folders and assets can be created, edited, deleted, and used as production material paths
evidence: Source files present: src/app/page.tsx, src/app/globals.css

2026-06-02 passed browser check on http://127.0.0.1:3001/: production module rendered production queue/review/draft library and did not render crawl task, keyword pool, or content pool management on desktop/mobile
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: External OpenAI/TikHub/Feishu calls are not part of the default baseline. Browser manual UI review is still recommended.

features.title: AI auto-tagging for crawled content and visual assets
definition_of_done: Crawled source items are automatically tagged with up to four allowed content tags before being persisted
The first nine source images or video highlight frames can receive one allowed visual tag each
Content and visual tags can be manually edited from the content-pool management UI
Manual tag edits are persisted through the existing SQLite-backed content item update path
evidence: 2026-06-03 video frame-first update: src/lib/source-tagging.ts now uses only videoFrames for video/mixed visual tagging when frames exist; src/app/page.tsx uses frames as the image preview list; npm run lint, tsc, build, local restart, and Trellis baseline passed
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: External GPT vision/text behavior is not part of the default baseline. Visual tagging sends available local media as inline image data where possible; if the relay rejects image inputs, visual tagging records a failed status without blocking content text tags.

features.title: Batch management for content pool and generated posts
definition_of_done: Users can multi-select content pool items and run bulk status or delete actions
Users can multi-select generated posts and run bulk status or delete actions
Batch APIs persist changes through the existing SQLite-backed domain stores
Batch actions record execution-log entries and refresh affected UI state
evidence: 2026-06-02 Playwright check confirmed visible selection controls: content-pool selection changes from 选择 to 已选, generated-post selection changes from 选择 to 已选, and batch counters update
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Bulk delete is destructive and requires browser confirmation. Manual browser walkthrough is still recommended before heavy operational use.

features.title: V1 whitelist account system with owner isolation
definition_of_done: The workspace supports small-team whitelist login from environment configuration
Whitelist users receive stable account ids for local owner attribution
Whitelist mode supports administrator users from WORKSPACE_ADMIN_USERS
Browser sessions use HttpOnly cookies and daily sign-in uses per-user hashed passwords
Members can access only their own owner-scoped runtime records while admins can access all records
Write paths attribute new simple runs, content, material entries, generated posts, logs, and Feishu publish jobs to the current account
Default verification covers account schema and wiring without creating real credentials
evidence: Source files present: src/lib/workspace-accounts.ts, src/app/api/accounts/route.ts, src/app/api/accounts/session/route.ts

2026-06-08 passed: node .trellis/verification/workspace_accounts_check.mjs; node .trellis/verification/link_import_check.mjs; node .trellis/verification/generated_posts_upsert_check.mjs; npx --no-install tsc --noEmit; npm run lint; npm run build; full powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .trellis/verification/check.ps1; npm run local:watch-simple -- -Once; npm run local:restart; read-only /api/accounts/session verification on http://127.0.0.1:3001
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Default whitelist accounts identify operators and partition app-layer runtime records by owner for normal members. Admins can see all records. Direct media URL authorization under /media and /generated remains a V1.1 hardening item. No real whitelist credentials or account-table credentials are created during automated verification.

features.title: High-throughput provider concurrency
definition_of_done: TikHub, media cache, GPT, image generation, Feishu, and production work use shared application-level concurrency pools
Default pool limits match current product targets: GPT 50, image 100, Feishu 50, with hard caps in code
Simple-mode platform crawling and post production run concurrently while progress writes remain serialized
Advanced batch production runs concurrently while task-status writes remain serialized
Generated post saves use single-row upsert so concurrent status updates do not duplicate primary keys
Baseline verification includes a static concurrency integration check without calling external services
evidence: 2026-06-08 collection/generation defaults raised while Feishu writes remain queued: crawl=12, media=30, production=30, SIMPLE_RUN_WORKER_CONCURRENCY=4, Feishu queue worker default=1 with per-owner serialization; concurrency and Feishu queue Trellis checks plus full baseline passed
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: Provider work still uses application-process concurrency pools. Simple-mode queued runs now use a durable database queue, but subtask-level provider replay and Feishu per-post idempotency remain future work. Real large-count simple runs still need manual validation before marking done.

features.title: Simple mode automatic content workflow
definition_of_done: Users can switch between simple and advanced workspace modes
Simple mode accepts keyword crawl, exact links, Feishu task numbers, one viral source link, or one original prompt, plus scoped target/count controls
Simple mode runs crawl, AI tagging, content production, image processing, approval, and Feishu publish as one backend sequence
Simple mode avoids unbounded image/keyframe processing per source item
Advanced mode can edit and save the default text and image prompts used by simple mode
Generated posts publish content tags to the Feishu Base 内容标签 multi-select field
evidence: Source files present: src/lib/simple-runs.ts, src/lib/workspace-settings.ts, src/lib/viral-replication.ts, src/app/api/simple/runs/route.ts, src/app/api/workspace/settings/route.ts

2026-06-30 simple-mode original generation added: UI/API accept sourceMode original with originalPrompt and default-off originalUseWebSearch; src/lib/original-creation.ts generates one platform=original post, plans up to 5 image prompts, uses the existing image provider path, auto-approves the post, and queues Feishu publish; focused checks simple_original_run_check.mjs, simple_link_run_check.mjs, simple_viral_run_check.mjs, simple_queue_check.mjs, tsc, and lint passed
Detailed historical evidence archived at .trellis/spec/fluxpost/archive/feature-list-history-2026-06-17.json
features.notes: External TikHub/OpenAI/image-generation/Feishu calls are not part of default baseline verification. A real small-count simple run and a larger two-keyword queue validation are still needed before marking done.

features.title: Reference and vehicle image library
definition_of_done: Signed-in users can import clipboard, file, drag-drop, and folder images into TOS-backed reference or vehicle views with shareable role URLs
Reference assets support structured AI tags, manual overrides, and durable retagging while vehicle assets use only user-maintained free-form tags and never enqueue AI by themselves
Fullscreen preview supports bounded navigation, zoom, pan, touch, focus management, keyboard controls, editing, and two-level deletion
Legacy vehicle images can be migrated idempotently without changing existing material or generation paths
Default verification covers both schemas and critical contracts without calling live TOS or GPT
evidence: Candidate implementation includes library assets/tagging APIs, authenticated role views, manual-only vehicle tags, no-AI vehicle imports, TOS persistence, and deterministic reference/vehicle checks
features.notes: Keep in_progress until the immutable candidate passes the 104 offline gate and real isolated TOS/GPT library scenarios before production promotion.

features.title: Volcengine TOS runtime media storage
definition_of_done: New crawl images, source videos, selected frames, generated images, review uploads, and Feishu-imported media can persist to TOS behind a default-off switch
Uploads use object-level public-read and are accepted only after HEAD length and ETag verification
Failed uploads retain recoverable bytes under data/tos-pending and can be reconciled idempotently without writing unverified business URLs
Historical local media URLs remain valid and file-only consumers safely materialize and clean HTTP media
Advanced configuration masks TOS credentials and admin-only checks validate public GET, video Range, and cleanup
The 82.158.226.10 deployment passes a real application media workflow while unrelated VPS services remain healthy
evidence: 2026-07-20 offline TOS runtime-media check, lint, type-check, build, full baseline, local restart, public config secrecy checks, route authorization checks, and responsive config-page screenshots passed
2026-07-20 isolated live TOS probe passed PUT, HEAD, anonymous GET, video Range 206, and DELETE without persisting or exposing operator credentials
features.notes: Implementation and isolated provider validation are complete. Keep ready_for_review until deployment to 82.158.226.10 and a real FluxPost image/video workflow prove URL persistence, local staging cleanup, historical media compatibility, and service health. The older VPS remains out of scope.
